content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Four amendments address error handling in daemon_loop.rs reconciliation paths. Two (fr-8-55c3e00a and fr-8-11232530) concern the mark_failed failure path and task stranding. Two (fr-8-b8161fe8 and fr-8-87f46a53) concern MilestoneUpdateFailed handling — one proposes making it a hard failure, the other proposes a repair mechanism. After code review, the lease-release and milestone-update concerns are both valid bugs with real consequences.

## Votes

- `fr-8-55c3e00a`: Accept
  Valid concern. When mark_failed fails at line 939 (or 1493 in the single-repo path), the lease is released (lines 948-949 / 1500-1501) but the task status was never durably updated from Active/Claimed. Phase 3 only scans Pending tasks (lines 548, 1029), and Phase 0 only handles label_dirty tasks (line 377). So the task sits in Active with no lease and no retry path. On the next cycle the task won't be picked up because it's not Pending and not label_dirty — it's actually stranded, not in an infinite loop. But the underlying problem (unreachable task state) is real and worth fixing. A bounded retry counter or sentinel file is a reasonable mitigation. The amendment is directionally correct even if its description of the exact failure mode (infinite loop vs stranding) depends on whether the task gets re-scanned — fr-8-11232530 gives the more precise diagnosis, but this amendment correctly identifies the fundamental problem.
- `fr-8-b8161fe8`: Accept
  Valid concern. The code at lines 1769-1783 intentionally downgrades MilestoneUpdateFailed to a warning and proceeds to mark_completed. The inline comment claims 'on retry record_bead_completion handles replays (idempotent lineage upsert)' — but there IS no retry, because mark_completed (line 953/1505) moves the task to Completed, removing it from all processing queues permanently. The milestone snapshot, journal state, and task-to-bead linkage from update_milestone_state (success_reconciliation.rs:148) are never written, and nothing will ever attempt them again. This creates a permanent inconsistency: the bead is closed in br, but the milestone shows it as in-progress with stale counts. The suggestion to add a milestone repair pass in Phase 0 (detecting closed beads with missing completion records) is practical and fits the existing architecture pattern where Phase 0 already handles deferred cleanup.
- `fr-8-87f46a53`: Reject
  While this amendment correctly identifies the same bug as fr-8-b8161fe8 (MilestoneUpdateFailed being swallowed leads to permanent partial reconciliation), its proposed fix — treating MilestoneUpdateFailed as a hard reconciliation failure — is the wrong solution. At the point MilestoneUpdateFailed fires, the critical bead mutation has already succeeded: close_bead_idempotent (line 139) and sync_after_close (line 145) have both completed. Making this a hard failure would cause mark_failed to run, which would mark the task as Failed and attempt to push a failed branch — even though the bead is already durably closed in br. On the next retry, reconciliation would attempt to close an already-closed bead (idempotent, OK) and sync again (OK), then hit the same milestone failure — creating an actual infinite failure loop if the milestone error is persistent, or needlessly failing a task whose core work succeeded. The fr-8-b8161fe8 approach (repair pass) is strictly better because it preserves the task's Completed status while providing a recovery path for the milestone data.
- `fr-8-11232530`: Accept
  This is the most precisely diagnosed amendment. When mark_failed fails at lines 916-946 / 1486-1498, the task's status was never durably written as Failed (task_service.rs:409 shows mark_failed does read_task → transition_to → write_task, and if write_task fails the state isn't persisted). The lease is then released (lines 948-949 / 1500-1501), leaving the task in Claimed or Active status with no lease holder. Phase 0 only processes label_dirty tasks (line 377), and Phase 3 only scans Pending tasks (lines 548, 1029). There is no recovery path for an unleased Claimed/Active task — it is permanently stranded. The fix is straightforward: when mark_failed itself errors, do NOT release the lease. The task will then be detected on the next daemon startup (or via lease expiry/cleanup) and can be retried. This preserves the existing invariant that non-terminal tasks should have active leases.
